name: The and Command
guide_hints:
# Level 10: The and Command

The `and` command is the counterpart to `left`/`right`. It is used for Conjunction Introduction.
If your goal is a conjunction `A ∧ B`, applying `and` (with no target) will split your goal into two separate sub-missions: one to prove `A`, and once that is proven, another to prove `B`.
If you have two proven premises `A` and `B`, you can type `and f1 f2` to combine them into a single proven premise `A ∧ B`.

### Instructions:
Create prop variables `p` and `q`.
Create the goal: `cf goal p ⇒ (q ⇒ (p ∧ q))`.
Start a mission: `mission goal` (premise `p`).
Start a sub-mission for the new goal: `mission goal` (premises `p` and `q`).

Now your goal is `p ∧ q`. Type `and`.
Your goal is now split! The active goal is `p`.
Use `apply goal PC1 p` to prove `p`. The REPL will then automatically switch your goal to `q`.

Use `apply goal PC1 q` to prove `q`.
Once both are proven, the `and` command finishes and your `p ∧ q` goal is proven! Use `auto` or `apply` to close out the remaining missions.
